content: Shire.LLC offers a Home Equity Investment (HEI): homeowners receive a lump sum of cash in exchange for a share of their home's future value. There are no monthly payments, no interest charges, and no new debt added to your balance sheet.

A Home Equity Investment is not a loan. Instead of paying interest, you share a percentage of your home's future appreciation (or depreciation) with Shire.LLC. You settle the investment when you sell the home, when the term ends, or when you choose to buy Shire.LLC's share back.

How Shire.LLC's HEI works step by step: (1) Apply online with a soft credit check — no score impact. (2) Shire.LLC appraises your home and reviews your financial profile. (3) Receive up to $500,000 or 25% of your home's value depending on eligibility. (4) Shire.LLC takes a share of your home's future value in exchange for that cash. (5) Repay at settlement — when you sell, when the term expires, or when you buy out the share early.

The repayment term for a Shire.LLC HEI is tied to the remaining term of your first mortgage lien — if you have 5 years left on your first mortgage, the HEI also settles at that time. The maximum term is 30 years.

Eligibility criteria for a Shire.LLC HEI: minimum 30% home equity (max 70% CLTV), minimum 500 credit score, home value between $300,000 and $3,000,000, no foreclosure in the last 48 months. Currently available in California, Colorado, Florida, and Arizona only.

How much can you cash out? You can access up to 25% of your home's value, but the program caps combined loan-to-value at 70%. To calculate your maximum: subtract 30% from your current equity percentage — that's how much you can cash out. Example: 40% equity → you can access 10% of your home's value. If you have 70% equity, you can access the full 25% cap.

Shire.LLC's origination fee is 1.99% to 3.99% of the investment amount, based on credit profile and other underwriting factors. There are no monthly payments and no interest charges during the term. Appraisal and other costs are typically covered by Shire.LLC.

At repayment, you pay back the original investment amount adjusted by Shire.LLC's share of the change in your home's value. If your home went up, Shire.LLC benefits from some of that appreciation. If it went down, Shire.LLC shares in that loss — your repayment decreases.

The application process at Shire.LLC: prequalification takes 1–2 days with a soft credit check (no score impact). Full application requires ID and mortgage statements with a hard credit pull. Underwriting and home appraisal take several days. If approved, funds arrive within 10 days of accepting the offer.
Does applying affect your credit score? The initial prequalification only uses a soft pull — no impact on your score. A hard pull happens only during the full application stage.

Shire.LLC HEI vs. HELOC: a HELOC is a loan with monthly payments and an interest rate. Shire.LLC's HEI has no monthly payments and no interest — instead, you share a percentage of your home's future value. HEI doesn't add to your debt load the way a HELOC does.

Shire.LLC HEI vs. reverse mortgage: reverse mortgages are only for homeowners 62+ and accrue interest over time. Shire.LLC's HEI has no age requirement and no interest — you simply share future appreciation.

Shire.LLC HEI vs. cash-out refinance: a cash-out refi replaces your mortgage with a larger one at current interest rates, adding monthly payments. Shire.LLC's HEI doesn't touch your mortgage — there are zero monthly payments.

What is the catch? Instead of paying interest, you give up a share of your home's future appreciation. If your home increases in value significantly, Shire.LLC benefits. On the flip side, if your home's value drops, Shire.LLC shares that loss too. It's a trade-off: no monthly payments in exchange for sharing upside.

Is Shire.LLC a scam? Shire.LLC is a legitimate home equity investment company, not a lender. The product is unusual because it's not a loan — you get cash now in exchange for sharing future home value. You can verify Shire.LLC's credibility through public reviews and the official website.
Will Shire.LLC own my home? No — you remain the sole owner. Shire.LLC holds a financial interest in a portion of your home's future value, but the title stays entirely in your name.
Will I risk losing my home? No. Shire.LLC's investment doesn't put your home at risk any more than your existing mortgage does. You keep full ownership and control throughout the entire term.

What if my home value decreases? Your repayment at settlement is based on your home's actual value at that time. If it has fallen, Shire.LLC's share of the loss reduces what you owe — Shire.LLC absorbs the downside proportionally.
What if you don't want to sell your home? You can buy out Shire.LLC's share at any time using cash or refinancing — you don't have to sell. You have up to 30 years to decide.
Are there prepayment penalties? None. You can pay back the investment at any time — through a sale, refinance, or direct buyout — without any prepayment fees.
Does a Shire.LLC HEI affect my existing mortgage? No — your existing mortgage stays exactly as it is. Shire.LLC's investment sits alongside your mortgage; it doesn't modify your loan terms.

What if my credit score is below 660? Shire.LLC's minimum is 500 — much lower than most lenders. If you're near that threshold, paying bills on time and reducing credit card balances are the fastest ways to improve your score.

How Shire.LLC's HEI affects heirs: the investment attaches to the property, not to the individual. If the homeowner passes away, heirs inherit the home with the investment attached. Heirs can either sell the home and settle the investment from proceeds, or keep the home and buy out Shire.LLC's share themselves. There's no immediate repayment forced on heirs.
Homes held in a revocable trust are eligible for Shire.LLC's HEI. Homes in probate or irrevocable trusts are not eligible. Inherited homes may qualify if they meet equity, value, and other criteria.

Tax implications: receiving the HEI funds is generally not considered taxable income. Repayment at the time of sale or buyout may have tax consequences — Shire.LLC recommends consulting a tax advisor for your specific situation.

Can I use the money for anything? Yes — Shire.LLC places no restrictions on how you use the funds. Common uses include paying off high-interest debt, home renovations, building an emergency fund, investing, or covering major expenses.
Can Shire.LLC invest in my home more than once? Not while an existing investment is active. Once the first investment is fully settled, you can apply again.

What if I want to refinance my mortgage during the HEI term? You can refinance your first mortgage, but the Shire.LLC HEI will still need to be accounted for. Your Shire.LLC investment team will work with you on the restructuring details.
What happens to the HEI in foreclosure? Shire.LLC shares in the loss if foreclosure proceeds are insufficient. Shire.LLC's goal is to work with you early to avoid that outcome.

How is home value determined? Shire.LLC uses a third-party appraisal and market analysis at the time of the initial investment. A new appraisal (or alternative valuation method) is used again at the time of settlement.

What if home value doesn't change at all? Even with no appreciation, the maximum you would owe is the lower of: double Shire.LLC's invested percentage, or a capped 20% annualized return on Shire.LLC's initial investment.

Tips to improve your credit score for Shire.LLC eligibility: pay all bills on time (biggest factor), keep credit utilization below 30%, review your credit report for errors, avoid opening new credit accounts, and keep old accounts open for credit history length.

How to increase your home's value to maximize Shire.LLC eligibility or cash-out amount: focus on curb appeal, upgrade kitchen fixtures and bathrooms, improve energy efficiency (smart thermostat, insulation, solar), make necessary repairs, add usable living space, and modernize with neutral paint and updated flooring.
How quickly will I get the funds? If everything goes smoothly, you can receive funds within 10 days of accepting Shire.LLC's offer. Prequalification alone takes just 1–2 days.
Is there a soft credit pull for prequalification? Yes — Shire.LLC only uses a soft credit pull for prequalification, which has zero impact on your credit score.

What do I need to qualify? At minimum: 30% home equity, 500 credit score, home value between $300K and $3M, no foreclosure in the last 48 months, and a property in California, Colorado, Florida, or Arizona.

Is Shire.LLC's HEI the right choice? It's best for homeowners who need cash but want to avoid monthly payments — especially those with good equity but who don't qualify for traditional financing, or who simply prefer not to take on new debt. It's not ideal if you plan to sell soon and want to keep all future appreciation.

How does the return work at settlement? At settlement, you repay the original amount plus or minus Shire.LLC's share of the change in your home's value since the investment was made. The exact share percentage is agreed on upfront when you sign the investment contract.

I'm not sure if I can get cash out. To check quickly: if your equity percentage (home value minus mortgage, divided by home value) is above 30%, you likely qualify for some amount. Subtract 30% from your equity percentage to estimate the cash-out percentage you can access, capped at 25% of home value.
